events.description: Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotted 159.67 acres (S½ NE¼, SE¼ NW¼, Lot 2, Section 19, T.9S., R.34E.) as Crow allottee No. 2336; trust patent issued December 2, 1907 under Act of February 8, 1887
Francis Geisdorff allotted 648.04 acres as homestead under Act of June 4, 1920; trust patent signed by President Calvin Coolidge on December 21, 1923; patent number 227584
Petition for Sale of Land by original allottee Frederick Geisdorff Jr. received at Crow Agency
Thomas R. Powers made an offer to lease Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment and adjacent land owned by relatives
Appraisal made by Government Farmer pertaining to Powers' request to lease land; appraised value $1,194
Crow Superintendent C.H. Asbury wrote to Frederick Geisdorff Sr. sending leasing papers and requesting answer
Crow Agency offered for sale the allotment of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. on petition acknowledged before Supt. Kneale of Uintah and Ouray Agency
Acceptance of sale of allotment sent to Frederick Geisdorff Jr. for his signature
Frederick Geisdorff Jr. signed and returned acceptance form to Crow office
Thomas R. Powers notified that owners of land under Crow Land Sale No. 991 signed written acceptance of his bid of $1,500 for 159.67 acres; balance of purchase price demanded
Checks for balance of sale price acknowledged as received by Superintendent; two checks from Powers for $1,350 and $2.00 (advertising fee) dated November 2, 1918 on Sheridan Banking Company
Superintendent wrote to Frederick Geisdorff Jr. noting he was only 20 years of age and doubting Indian Bureau would approve sale; requested birth date and names of relatives
Frederick Geisdorff Jr. died of influenza aged about 20 years, married, intestate; survived by wife Emily J. Geisdorff and minor daughter Emily Lucile Geisdorff
Superintendent Kneale wrote to Crow Agency notifying of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s death from influenza
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Thomas R. Powers informing him of age issue and offering to cancel sale and return payment
Mrs. Emily J. Geisdorff wrote to Crow Agency Superintendent inquiring about aid in paying note owed by her deceased husband
Superintendent answered widow's letter regretting no funds available; mentioned pending sale uncertainty due to allottee's age; advised hearing to determine heirs
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Commissioner of Indian Affairs reviewing all matters concerning land sale; holding two checks from Powers for $1,350 and $150; allottee born May 6, 1898
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Mrs. Emily M. Geisdorff enclosing copy of letter to Commissioner; asked if she wished pending sale completed
Uintah and Ouray Agency notified Supt. C. Asbury of Crow Agency of birth of Emily Lucile Geisdorff, born May 23, 1918
Office of Indian Affairs responded to Asbury's March 24 letter; stated nothing to show brother/sister want to sell or widow wants sale; if heirs want sale to proceed, prepare and forward sale papers
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Mrs. Emily F. Geisdorff asking whether she wished sale completed to Powers for $1,500 or bid rejected; enclosed authority to put old sale through
Superintendent Asbury enclosed sale papers to Commissioner stating he had just received acceptance of widow of allottee
General Land Office stated no reservations or withdrawals covering land; no reasons why patent in fee should not be issued to purchaser; signed by John McPhaul, Acting Assistant Commissioner
Fee patent No. 719146 issued to Thomas R. Powers for 159.67 acres of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment No. 2336; signed by President Woodrow Wilson
Office of Indian Affairs transmitted fee patent No. 719146 to Crow Agency for delivery to Thomas R. Powers upon signed receipt
Crow Agency notified U.S. Land Office Billings of fee patent No. 719146 issued November 14, 1919 to Thomas R. Powers on allotment No. 2336 of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.
Crow Agency wrote to Mrs. Emily J. Geisdorff informing her that after heirs determined, proceeds of sale can be transferred to her Agency upon request
Hearing held to determine heirs of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.; testimony taken by Superintendent Kneale of Fort Duchesne, Utah
Report on heirship transmitted to Secretary of Interior; heirs determined as wife Emily J. Geisdorff and daughter Emily Lucile Geisdorff under Utah statutes

Heirs of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. determined; check No. 10613 for $1,317.26 sent to Emily J. Geisdorff representing her share; $188.90 credited to daughter Emily; $15.00 deducted for heirship fee; total on books $1,521.24
Report prepared analyzing the Geisdorff land sale as potentially illegal; argued land should have reverted to Crow Tribe upon allottee's death under trust patent terms and Treaty of 1968

financial_transactions.payee: Estate of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. / Crow Agency
financial_transactions.description: Purchase price for 159.67 acres of allotment No. 2336; paid via two checks on Sheridan Banking Company: $1,350.00 and $150.00 deposit previously submitted with bid
Advertising fee for Crow Land Sale No. 991; check on Sheridan Banking Company
Heirship fee deducted from estate proceeds as authorized by Indian Office
Emily J. Geisdorff's share of land sale proceeds (one half plus dower interest); check No. 10613; total on books was $1,521.24 less $15.00 heirship fee
Minor daughter Emily Lucile Geisdorff's share of land sale proceeds; held to credit of her account
Bank account balance derived from lease money deposited to credit of estate in Merchants National Bank of Billings, Montana
Appraised value of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s 159.67-acre allotment as appraised by Government Farmer
Price per acre for 159.67 acres; total purchase price $1,500
